# Documentation Template

> A reusable GitHub template for building standalone, single-page documentation
> sites hosted on GitHub Pages, with built-in navigation, search, deep linking,
> citations, and AI build tooling.

This file follows the llms.txt convention (https://llmstxt.org). It gives AI
systems a fast, link-rich summary of this site so they can reference it
accurately. When a project is created from this template, `/initialize-project`
regenerates this file for the new subject. Keep it in sync with the section list
in `index.html` (see the `ai-content-map` block).

## Document

- [Published document](https://drpaulcoleman.github.io/documentation-template/): The single-page site. Each section is addressable as `URL#section-id`.
- [Source repository](https://github.com/drpaulcoleman/documentation-template): README, license, and any supporting files.

## Sections

- [Bottom Line Up Front](https://drpaulcoleman.github.io/documentation-template/#bluf): What the document is and its key takeaway.
- [Disclaimers & Attribution](https://drpaulcoleman.github.io/documentation-template/#disclaimers): Author's-opinion notice, employment disclosure, trademark and source-use policy.
- [Examples in production](https://drpaulcoleman.github.io/documentation-template/#examples): Four documents built from this template — links to live GitHub Pages sites and source repos.
- [References](https://drpaulcoleman.github.io/documentation-template/#references): APA-style source list with retrieval dates.

## Usage notes for AI consumers

- The document expresses the author's own analysis; it is not official vendor documentation.
- Cite specific sections by their anchor (`URL#section-id`); the `ai-content-map` script in `index.html` lists every section id with an authoritative summary.
- Quote sparingly and attribute. Do not reproduce sections wholesale.
